key_items: Bernstein: Bitcoin miners becoming critical suppliers in AI infrastructure with 27 GW planned power and $90B in AI deals
Data shows dip buyers waiting for lower prices with USDC exchange inflows spiking at $350M during pullback
SEC tokenized stock exemption expected this week; Japan Bitcoin ETF plans launching; regulatory environment shifting favorable
reasoning: Bitcoin faces short-term consolidation pressure around $76K-$77K as traders defensively position for a potential dip to $70K, creating near-term weakness. However, the macro backdrop is increasingly bullish: miners securing strategic AI infrastructure value, institutional accumulation via USDC inflows, favorable regulatory developments (SEC tokenization exemption, Japan ETFs), and corporate treasury accumulation (MSTR, Strive) all support a medium-term rally trajectory that should overcome current correction pressure.
risks: Heavy ETF outflows exceeding $1B combined with weakening support at $76K could trigger a cascade liquidation toward $70K if macro risk sentiment deteriorates or equities sell off sharply.
